Stuff I missed

Ibn Khaldun

We discussed Nacirema 

Enigaldi-Nanna first museum curator

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ennigaldi-Nanna%27s_museum

==Second lecture==

I’m a bit confused about the demographics
He claim to have needed to trade or people would resent each other
Does he know this well? Or is it just conjecture.
I’m interested in how they remembered the genealogies


==Haitian movie==




==September 21, 2023==

teacher beat Majora's mask when lacking things to do in field work

Composite people are sometimes used,
===We can use machine translation for sources===
==September 26==
Specialized methods
Life history. A qualitative in depth description of a person's life given to the narrator

Are photos acceptable?

[[Category:College]]
[[sn:Cultural Anthropology]][[sn:Cultural Anthropology]][[Category:Cultural Anthropology]]
===Ethics===
Ethical issues can exist in fieldwork

There was a Project Camelot American military program that basically had anthropologists acting as spies

Boas used potlatches to get stuff from people

Boas used photographers to distract people so he could do grave robberies

"[[Salvage anthropology]]" didn't question why the people were going away but said people needed to study it

==Photos==

Kings of Hawaii presented themselves in Western clothing

Many such people presented themselves in different ways than we'd think as shown in the lecture slides

Edward Curtis was a crazy person who acted really partronizing to people

Boas invented museum dioramas

==Ethics==

Avoid harm or wrong

Respect the wellbeing of humans and nonhuman anthropology

Don't obstruct other researchers

Honest publishing

Payment can always be an issue
==Exam structure==
Exam has a structure where there will be short answer true or false questions which are considered to have a true or false answer but you need to explain it and if you explain the wrong answer well enough then you will get two points

==Five Scapes of Globalization==
ethnoscapes, technoscapes, ideoscapes, financescapes, and mediascapes

==Kinship==
Kinship has a lot of ambiguities. It is rarely super well defined

==Mosuo==
Mosuo women do walking marriages
==Chiefs==
Chief is an institutional role which is often inherited or elected
==Inca system==
Three things built on every site
* Administrative site
* Temple of the sun 
* Acllawasi - House of women

==Ongka's big Moka==
Ongka has cool clothes

* Subsistence system: Horticulture or Pastoral
* Organization of settlements/size of population: Small sedentary villages
*Political system and subtype (need more info): above band system but below chiefdom (Tribe)
*Individuals live in houses in small villages
*Big man has no authority, he is highly persuasive instead
*Moka is the interest paid on a gift
*Pigs are important
*Ongka's power is limited by people wanting to go along with him
*Didn't really catch much explicitly about gender but the society seemed to be patriarchal and sex segregated.
*Clothing was inreresting and the differences in clothing formality levels

==Hunting life hypothesis==
Men hunted women gathered. IT is more a legitimizing ideology than an actual serious hypothesis

== Religion ==
Animism is anime, everything has a personhood

Animatism is animated, stuff like mana is impersonal

=== Types of practitioners ===

# Priests
# Shamans
# Prophets

=== Priests ===
Organized education

often men

set rules and control access to religious rites

=== Shamans ===
Part time. 

More likely to be women

Often includes spiritual healing or warfare

skill is inherited or learned, might be marked for the path

relies on achieving altered state of consciousness through dreams drugs music and dance

=== Prophets ===
Unique person who can communicate divine messages to others
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
